root: ^The first week was over. Arkady was lying in his bed, exhausted.
^Each morning he was taken with the other prisoners to the mines - endless shifts, bitter cold, men in silence breaking stone with their pickaxes.
^The food in the camp mess was terrible
^But it was so scarce that thoughts of it never left Arkady's mind.
^He started thinking about a piece of bread he would hide in his trousers next time, so he could eat it before going to sleep.
^Arkady heard someone enter the barracks. It was Boris Orlov.
^We need to talk. The glove that was stolen last week - we have to find it.

g-0: ^You probablu know that Andreyev used to be a terrorist. He organized the assassinations of imperial ministers, even members of the Tsar’s family. That was forty years ago.
^He led the Combat Organization of the Socialist Revolutionaries. People who remember those days speak of him with... respect.

s: ^You never said how you came to know him.
^And? What does it have to do with the glove?

c-0: ^Andreyev was my mentor. I joined SR in 1917. When the Revolution erupted, we all became fighters, and lost the fight, and I… Nevermind.
